title: Fattoush
excerpt: Ingredients 500 ml 1⁄2 cm cubes cucumber 750 ml 1⁄2 cm cubes tomatoes (use all sorts of colours together) 2 green sweet peppers, also cut into 1⁄2 cm cubes 2 red sweet peppers, also cut into 1⁄2 cm cubes 1 large white onion, also cut into 1⁄2 cm cubes 6 spring onions, cut into thin rings with the green stems and all …
html:
Ingredients

  • 500 ml 1⁄2 cm cubes cucumber
  • 750 ml 1⁄2 cm cubes tomatoes (use all sorts of colours together)
  • 2 green sweet peppers, also cut into 1⁄2 cm cubes
  • 2 red sweet peppers, also cut into 1⁄2 cm cubes
  • 1 large white onion, also cut into 1⁄2 cm cubes
  • 6 spring onions, cut into thin rings, green stems and all
  • 250 ml chopped flat-leaf parsley
  • 250 ml chopped mint leaves
  • 200 ml olive oil
  • finely grated zest of a lemon
  • 90 ml fresh lemon juice
  • 65 ml honey
  • 2 – 4 crushed cloves garlic
  • salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
  • a few toasted flatbreads (pita breads)

Method

Put all the vegetables and herbs in a large mixing bowl.

In a bottle with a screw-top lid, shake together the olive oil, lemon zest and juice, honey, garlic
and salt and black pepper to taste.

Pour over the vegetables and mix thoroughly.

Tear the flatbreads into small pieces and mix through the vegetables.

Cover the bowl and let the Fattoush stand for an hour or so at room temperature before serving so the flavours can develop.

Serves 8

Serve with grilled chicken, meat or fish.

Easy!
